blocking_issues: PyTorch dependency not specified in setup.py

completeness.reasoning: MISALIGNED NOTEBOOKS pattern: The core LoRA method is well-implemented in loralib/*.py files, but scanner will exclude ALL .py files due to .ipynb presence and instead select irrelevant HuggingFace transformer tutorials that don't demonstrate LoRA at all.

clarity.reasoning: LoRA library has good docstrings and clear API, but training examples require external data configuration and have some hardcoded assumptions.

modularity.reasoning: LoRA library (loralib) is cleanly packaged and importable independently. Well-structured with clear separation between layers, utils, and examples.

reproducibility.reasoning: pip install loralib works but setup.py doesn't specify PyTorch dependency. No custom CUDA compilation needed.

executability.reasoning: No demo runs the core LoRA method without external data. Training scripts require --train_data and --valid_data arguments. Available notebooks demonstrate HuggingFace transformers, not LoRA.

recommendations: CRITICAL: Add a simple demo notebook that imports loralib and demonstrates LoRA layer usage with synthetic data
Move LoRA demonstration notebooks to root level so scanner finds them instead of peripheral HuggingFace content
Create a minimal working example that doesn't require external dataset downloads
Add sample data files that demonstrate LoRA fine-tuning on a small included dataset
